review_title: Flexible, Powerful Tool with a Learning Curve
description: Overall, using Qualtrics XM has been a positive experience. Mostly I and my team used it for collecting basic data, however, when I had the opportunity to use it for research data collection, I was impressed with the flexibility of the settings and logic to deploy a high-level, IRB-approved survey. It just took some time to learn it, and that's not necessarily a bad thing.
author: Ginger S.
pros: Qualtrics XM is a powerful, flexible tool that can perform funtions as basic as a survey on lunch preferences to research surveys with IRB-required anonymity. The variety of question types, delivery methods, collaboration options, and export settings make Qualtrics a versatile tool for a wide variety of settings.
cons: Due to its capacity for high-level performance, many of the tools, options, and settings are not intuitive. Significant time and effort along with thorough training are needed to make use of all the features available to Qualtrics XM users.

review_title: By far the best survey platform out there.
description: Very positive--not major complaints, and the customer service team is also very knowledgable and timely in responding to your resquests.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: By far the best survey platform out there. A comprehensive suite inclusive of survey design, survey outreach, panel management, and analytics.
cons: The biggest pain point is that you cannot tag your panelists to different topics. You can only overwrite existing information.

review_title: Good product held by poor support and reporting options
description: Good tool for collection data, mediocre (at best) for reporting and analysis of results. Terrible customer support - I cannot stress this enough
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Advanced survey logic, routing, skip logic. Advanced question types, workflows, survey distribution methods
cons: Poor customer service. Poor reliability, changes nad updates are made in a haphazard manner. Reporting (even the Results Dashboard) is lacking in functionality.

review_title: XM for Strategy
description: it is considered a powerful tool for market research and strategic insights generation across various industries.
author: Tahir A.
pros: I like the was we can see how the research is communicated
cons: There could be more indepth information given on the research

review_title: XM- an ultimate choice for all of your research automation works
description: Overall it was great and value for money for all R&D activities
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Extremely liner backend language used which makes it a great tool that never breaks down. The advanced AI which helps to bridge between Logical and numerical worlds. It helps a novice or end user to make sense of the effect of the extraneous variable which is been tested under.
cons: The set up takes a lot of cache memory and hijack the speed of the processor. If you are using a lot of multilayered applications at once, you are prone to face some disruptions for sure. Another what I feel is the dashboard. It needs to be more advance at least the vertical limit options should be more enriched.

review_title: Designed to Advance your metrics
description: This tools is fantastic for working with our Faculty and Researchers. They expect in-depth analysis and appreciate the power of Qualtrics. Our staff and administrative personnel have all learned to use Qualtrics and most of my co-workers find it easy to use and can adapt it to their various needs. This is a great option for anyone looking for a variety of analytics for surveying, and can be adapted to simpler projects as well.
author: Andrew P.
pros: The most useful part of Qualtrics is the depth of analytics you receive on your surveys, questionnaires, and other tools. In real time, as you develop your surveys you are given insights into how your data can be analyzed. It is designed to help you get the data you need back without asking unnecessary questions. The menu for building out surveys is very easy to use and the number of options and variety in types of questions gives you the ability to develop them exactly how you want. Conditional formatting is easy to implement, and there are many ways to incorporate other media into your survey/questionnaire. The surveys are built to be compatible in mobile without any additional design input as well.
cons: At times Qualtrics is a more powerful tool than you need. I find that there are some users who adapt to Qualtrics quickly, but others simply do not need it. If you are asking straight-forward surveys and only need basic analysis, you can find easier and lower cost tools. Some people can be overwhelmed by the options and the "analysis" that come with the variety of question-types. That being said, this can be a one-size-fits-all solution, but there will be some training and trial and error involved.

review_title: Data Collection and Analysis Made Easy
description: We have used Qualtrics and SurveyMonkey for larger surveys (50-100 questions) about students' experiences and Qualtrics makes manipulating the date very simple. It's easy to filter and export the data if you want to use Excel to do a bit more digging.
author: Nadir S.
pros: I primarily use SurveyMonkey, Google Forms, and Qualtrics for surveying my students. Qualtrics is adopted by our University on a wider scale and we have access to all features. Qualtrics is a very capable, easy to use tool. There are a lot of helpful resources online that can be used to make the most of the features.
cons: If you are looking for a short and quick form to collect simple data, like signups, that don't require any data analysis, it is often easier to set it up in Google Forms or O365 than in Qualtrics. Qualtrics needs many more clicks for a simple survey than other (free) options.

review_title: Has most of what you need, missing a lot of extensive options for customization
description: Overall we like Qualtrics and it gives us everything we need to draft and send out surveys for our company
author: Connor C.
pros: I like how it is optimized for creating normal surveys. For most of your projects, you don’t need to spend extra time on things that should be easy because they made it easy to do those things.
cons: There are not enough customization options for some of our projects, and when using personalized links, the survey expires a week or so after being opened with no ability to change the duration before it expires

review_title: Simplify data collection from target groups directly
description: Qualtrics XM made setting up and publishing surveys easier and faster, enabling us to expand our marketing research deployment and continuously feed the database.
author: Shir H.
pros: Qualtrics XM provides us with excellent services. We can create any survey, I loved the ease of integrating it into social media ads or promotional messages across all communication channels. I loved how Qualtrics XM makes the questions within the form clear and organized and enables respondents to navigate and record answers easily. I loved its ability to screen respondents and ensure they are real and from within the target group.
cons: There are no negatives at all. With Qualtrics XM everything is easy and data collection is straightforward.

review_title: Fantastic tool
description: Overall XM makes collecting and visualizing data super easy. The interface makes it easy to construct complex questions that have many layers to them; with all the features and options they provide users, surveys are far easier to execute than with other products.
author: Renee L.
pros: XM has a great offering of features with their tool, I think they lead the industry with this. The data visualization options and the survey builder makes composing complex surveys and sharing the data a breeze.
cons: The only negative I can really say is the cost for XM. It's a fantastic tool and I understand that the subscription price is a reflection of that.

review_title: Excellent survey tool
author: Amr A.
pros: It's user friendly and very easy to set up surveys
cons: The expert review score can sometime be confusing

review_title: It's the best - but the future is concerning
description: Qualtrics is excellent all around, and has been the standard survey software among academic researchers for years for a reason. However, while the base platform is great, the organization is far from perfect. I had numerous issues deploying the offline app. After months of contacting support -- and getting zero explanation as to why my account, which was enabled for the offline app, could not access the app. After months of bothering them, they did something -- didn't tell me what -- and it worked. Their support team, while friendly, wasn't knowledgeable about their survey app and couldn't connect me with someone who was. Their best information regarding app crashes was that "the development team says it's buggy." Not super helpful considering the additional fee for the app. The timing of these experiences with support -- the same time that the Qualtrics company was sold, and immediately refocused toward the "customer experience" and CRM products they make -- has me concerned about the future of Qualtrics for academic research.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Building and deploying a basic survey in Qualtrics is very easy. Every step of the process, from writing questions to survey launch and distribution will be simple as long as you allow it to be. For advanced users, Qualtrics offers a number of methods for building in complex survey logic (such as skip patterns, display patterns, and survey flow mapping). Additionally, the built-in question types can be modified using JavaScript, allowing advanced users to ensure that the question "works" exactly as designed, without compromise. Sending the survey is exceedingly easy as long as you've got someone to send it to. Followups with those who haven't respond can be automated through their email system as well. Exporting data to a variety of formats upon survey completion is simple. As long as you've done everything right within Qualtrics, the quality of the data you export should be pretty high.
cons: The data analysis options offered within Qualtrics are weak, as are the data cleaning options. This is a non-issue for smaller, or non-scientific surveys. However, for most of its existence Qualtrics has been licensed by universities worldwide specifically for use in scientific studies. Less experienced researchers may be misled by these lacking tools and produce inaccurate analyses based on them. The Qualtrics survey platform also offers an offline application -- for an additional fee -- which is extremely weak. It does not work with many functions that a survey used primarily online may include, such as the "request response" option. It's also prone to crashing for no clear reason. Users dealing with protected health information (i.e. those who need to be HIPAA compliant) should consider other options. While Qualtrics does consider themselves "self-assessed" to be HIPAA compliant, they do not enter business associate agreements with clients (so, they do not take liability for the protection of the data); other platforms, such as SurveyMonkey, offer full HIPAA compliance with a BAA.

review_title: Best of the best but super pricey
description: Superb tool for conducting market, academic, behavioural, or business research. Very feature rich and easy to learn. Overall great experience but just super pricey for all but the bigger companies.
author: Eshwar S.
pros: Feature-rich and easy to learn. Integrates with lots of software given it's the industry standard. UI is really nice to use and offers tools for analysis too.
cons: Extremely pricey. They offer discounts if you ask, especially for smaller companies. But the plans shut out a lot of people. Free trial is pretty good giving you all features but time limited; so maybe do this to start with.Also the plans are odd; all plans except the top (very pricey custom quote plan) limit you to 1000 responses per year, so even with a really nice plan you are quite limited response wise. Particularly bad if you run frequent surveys.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Qualtrics is a Fantastic Product
description: It is a fantastic product that is continuing to evolve over time.
author: Sriram C.
pros: Qualtrics is the tool I depend on for collecting data from target sample groups. The Qualitrics app is easy to use for building surveys, collecting data, and interpreting results using automatically generated reports, with graphs and tables.
cons: I have zero complaints about Qualtrics. The app is great for maintaining privacy and security of user data and for managing personal identifying information.

review_title: Great tool for staff, students, etc.!
description: I've used qualtrics in many ways. For simple things like a door decorating contest among staff, a chili cook off registration, sign up for skydiving club, gathering information for students i advised, event registration and more. The trigger email is my favorite thing qualtrics has so follow up can be automated! Being able to copy qualtrics or edit ones you use frequently to fit your current needs are amazing, so you dont always have to start from scratch!
author: Kayla S.
pros: Qualtrics made it easy for me to gather information on students I advised. I could ask them questions related to their testing and if it was completed, what courses they were interested in taking, etc. and it made it so I didn't have to manually look all this info up (although I'd check their answers when we met!). It also made it possible for visits and events to have students sign themselves up as well as guests with a trigger email going to both of them from our team letting them know who to contact with further questions and attach a map of our campus!
cons: No cons, this system is one of the best that our institution has spent money on! It helps save time and make things efficient when we need to gather info!

review_title: Qualtrics Gets It
description: We leverage the Qualtrics Research Platform to drive key decisions for the overall direction of our organization, assess the needs and well being of those associated to our organization and on and on. We truly cover the full spectrum of use with this product and it has been absolutely critical in the decision making process and in helping us drive forward in our work. Qualtrics simply has it together.
author: Ryan O.
pros: Qualtrics is well run from top to bottom. Their products are excellent, intuitive and are capable of supporting customers of all variations. Unlike so many companies that get one thing right and then drop the ball in another area, Qualtrics excels at every level. Their software is excellent, their integration support and API is excellent and their support is hands down the absolute best in the tech industry. There's really no question in my mind why they were gobbled up before they could complete their IPO.
cons: Because I typically have to deal with low level interaction with Qualtrics (APIs etc.) my ONLY complaint is that they have a lot of undocumented features. However, their support is THE BEST and they always help me through finding those features when I need them.

review_title: Excellent Platform
description: In this day and age, having mobile-friendly administration of data is key. This massive organization uses many data collection products/vendors, but Qualtrics is the only that I know of which works mobile administration into processes so easily
author: Chad Ian P.
pros: The analytics options for performing certain data manipulations in the system before exporting data makes developing reports a very quick process. I love the chat-based support function as well.
cons: I find it odd that when removing (decrementing) cases from data sets, as I always do to remove preview data, that the time that the system takes to update numbers is rather long. I chatted with product support one day and was given a work-around (i.e., add an embedded data survey block in survey flows), but is still odd.

review_title: Great survey software!
description: Overall, I found that Qualtrics is a solid survey program. It can do just about anything that you would want in a survey program. It has plenty of features and it is easy to use. If you get stuck on something, their customer service will walk you through it. They also have a lot of great literature for you to find the answer yourself.
author: Debbie L.
pros: This is probably the most popular survey program on the market and for good reasons. It has the ability to design surveys with a ton of options for questions -- multiple choice, short answer, longer answer. You can randomize questions, organize the survey by blocks, use decision trees. It is easy to use and the customer service is amazing. They will also recruit panels of participants for you. It is easy to download the data into excel or other usable formats.
cons: I haven't found much yet. I am still learning how to use it, but most things are pretty intuitive. The one thing I would say that I don't like is that even though they will recruit panels of participants for you that match particular characteristics, they are relatively pricey.

review_title: A powerful survey tool with useful bolt-ons
description: Performed admirably as a survey tool but reporting and analysis didn't meet our needs and cost ultimately led us to switch platform again.
author: Mark N.
pros: Comprehensive survey build and management tool
cons: Reporting and analysis tools not as easy to use / powerful

review_title: Qualtrics surveys offer a good experience.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It’s easy to take surveys on this site. There is a large variety of options.
cons: I don’t have any issues with this software. It rarely, if ever, crashes.

review_title: Qualtrics CoreXM
description: I used Qualtircs for over one year now. Coming from a REDCap background, I miss some of the program's features compared to QUaltircs. Qualtrics is not as intuitive for the new user and takes twice as long to build and test surveys. The reports feature of Qualitrics has multiple glitches and is not as viewer-friendly when trying to look at data within the platform.
author: Brigitte M.
pros: I do not like this program. I do not have anything about it.
cons: I do not like the survey creation format of this product. It is not intuitive, and there is no easy way to make mass changes. It takes multiple steps to update the values of the multiple-choice options manually.

review_title: Essential Tool in the Research Arsenal
author: Nate C.
pros: I am a research-active academic who frequently conducts research via both survey and experimental methods. Qualtrics is an essential tool in this process. I found the learning curve to be gentle, allowing me--without prior experience or time spent reading detailed instruction--to quickly assemble surveys with various if/then branches and other formatting needs without much time or trouble. The same is true of my experimental work, with the Qualtrics platform allowing me to easily randomly assign participants to different versions of my surveys. Despite the ease of use, the platform also allows some pretty powerful customization using custom coding and formatting. I've taken advantage of these additional advanced features in the past and have been impressed. As opposed to some other competitors in this space, I also like the Qualtrics branding stays in the background when participants follow the link. Custom branding is allowed so that your own branding can appear on the landing pages.
cons: I know it may not be particularly helpful, but in my experience using this platform I have yet to find something that I dislike.

review_title: Research at it's best
description: Analysis of user adoption of software applications in a variety of fields
author: Henri A.
pros: Easy way to insights and good quality of graphical visualization and wealth of statistical constructs
cons: Missing graphical représentations in dashboards

review_title: Must have software for research questionnaires and more
description: I use this software for a variety of purposes, online questionnaires, registration for events, and submitting applications are a few examples. I've found it really useful and helps me get my job done more effectively.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Qualtrics is intuitive, organizes results for you, and helps with data analysis. You can export data in many different formats, and create just about any type of questionnaire imaginable. I embed videos, use skip and display logic, and you can email people their surveys from the software.
cons: There are only a few things that we have found the software incapable of doing, and they have been rather minor. We did request that the company add them to their "to-do" list. Customer support is really helpful when you run into problems.

review_title: Best in Class survey software
description: My overall experience has been positive.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This software is very user-friendly and powerful. It can do almost anything you like. In addition, the support is very helpful in terms of letting you know how to get some of the advanced features working.
cons: Working collaboratively at the same time with a colleague is not possible.
